text.title: accessibility-first-design Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for accessibility-first-design as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: LLMs hallucinating WCAG criterion numbers (e.g., citing "WCAG 1.4.13" for an issue that is actually 1.4.11).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agents are prone to criterion number errors. Constrain to a pre-validated rule list and require the agent to cite the exact SC number and name. Reviewers will catch and reject incorrect citations.

failure-mode.description: Estimating contrast ratios from screenshots rather than extracting hex values from tokens or the DOM.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Screenshot-based contrast estimation is unreliable due to JPEG compression, rendering engine differences, and anti-aliasing. Always extract actual color values programmatically.

failure-mode.description: Generating false positives for "color not the only indicator" on icons that already have adjacent labels.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agents apply this rule mechanically without understanding that a color-coded icon with a text label already satisfies SC 1.4.1. Provide context including adjacent element content.
